cards.prompt: A weak reference does not prevent collection: after a GC the target is gone (Target -> null)
cards.code: using System; static WeakReference Make(){ return new WeakReference(new byte[500000]); } static bool StillThere(WeakReference wr){ return (wr.Target as byte[]) != null; } var wr = Make(); bool before = StillThere(wr); GC.Collect(); GC.WaitForPendingFinalizers(); GC.Collect(); bool after = StillThere(wr); Console.WriteLine($"beforeGC={before} afterGC={after}");

cards.prompt: Obtaining a strong reference via Target prevents collection: the object stays alive
cards.code: using System; var o = new object(); var wr = new WeakReference(o); object strong = wr.Target; o = null; GC.Collect(); GC.WaitForPendingFinalizers(); GC.Collect(); bool stillAlive = wr.Target != null; GC.KeepAlive(strong); Console.WriteLine($"strongRefKeepsAlive={stillAlive}");

cards.prompt: Short weak reference (parameterless ctor) vs long (second arg true): TrackResurrection distinguishes them
cards.code: using System; var wShort = new WeakReference(new object()); var wLong = new WeakReference(new object(), true); Console.WriteLine($"shortTracksResurrection={wShort.TrackResurrection} longTracksResurrection={wLong.TrackResurrection}");
